Value.label: Minimum brightness level
Value.Help: Parameter is set automatically during the calibration process. Options for changing parameter 1-98. Default 1.

Value.label: Maximum brightness level
Value.Help: Parameter is set automatically during the calibration process. Options for changing parameter 2-99. Default 99.

Value.label: Incandescence level of dimmable compact fluorescent lamps
Value.Help: Virtual value set as a percentage level between parameters MIN (1%) and MAX. (99%). The Dimmer will set to this value after first switch on. It is required for warming up and switching dimmable compact fluorescent lamps and certain types of light sources. Options for changing parameter 1-99. Default 1.

Value.label: Incandescence time of dimmable compact fluorescent lamps
Value.Help: This parameter determines the time required for switching compact fluorescent lamps and certain types of light sources. Setting this parameter to 0 will disable the incandescence functionality. Available settings: 0-255 (0s - 25,5s)

Value.label: The percentage of a dimming step at automatic control
Value.Help: Available settings: 1-99 Default: 1

Value.label: Time of a dimming step at automatic control
Value.Help: Available settings: 0-255 (0s - 2,55s) Default setting: 1

Value.label: The percentage of a dimming step at manual control
Value.Help: Available settings: 1-99 Default: 1

Value.label: Time of a dimming step at manual control
Value.Help: Available settings: 0-255 (0s - 2,55s) Default setting: 1

Value.label: Saving state before power failure
Value.Help: Saving state before power failure. Default 1.

Item.label: State NOT saved at power failure, all outputs are set to OFF upon power restore
State saved at power failure, all outputs are set to previous state upon power restore

Value.label: Timer functionality (auto - off)
Value.Help: Available settings: 0 - Function disabled; 1-32767 - time to turn off measured in seconds (1s - 9,1h) Default setting: 0

Value.label: Enable/Disable ALL ON/OFF
Value.Help: Enable/Disable ALL ON/OFF. Default 255.

Item.label: ALL ON active / ALL OFF active
ALL ON disabled/ ALL OFF disabled
ALL ON disabled/ ALL OFF active
ALL ON active / ALL OFF disabled

Value.label: Force auto-calibration
Value.Help: Changing value of this parameter will force the calibration process.

Item.label: Start auto-calibration of the load without Fibaro Bypass
Start auto-calibration of the load with Fibaro Bypass

Value.label: Auto-calibration status
Value.Help: This parameter determines operating mode of the Dimmer (automatic/manual settings).

Item.label: calibration procedure not performed or Dimmer operates on manual settings

Value.label: Burnt out bulb detection
Value.Help: Function based on the sudden power variation of a specific value, interpreted as a LOAD ERROR. Available settings: 0 - function disabled; 1-99 - percentage value of power variation, compared to standard power consumption, measured during the calibration procedure (to be interpreted as load error/burnt out bulb) Default setting: 30

Value.label: Time delay of a burnt out bulb
Value.Help: Time of delay (in seconds) for power variation detection, interpreted as a LOAD ERROR or OVERLOAD detection (too much power connected to the Dimmer). Available settings: 0 - detection of a burnt out bulb disabled; 1-255 - delay time in seconds; Default setting: 5

Value.label: Forced switch on brightness level
Value.Help: If the parameter is active, switching on the Dimmer (S1 single click) will always set this brightness level. Available settings: 0 - function disabled; 1-99 - percentage level of brightness; Default setting: 0

Value.label: Inputs Button/Switch configuration
Value.Help: Binary inputs type configuration. Default 0.

Item.label: Role blind switch (UP / DOWN) - two switch keys operate the Dimmer

Value.label: The value sent to associated devices on single click
Value.Help: The value sent to associated devices on single click.

Item.label: 0xFF value is sent, which will set associated devices to their last saved state.
Current Dimmer state is sent, which will synchronize brightness level of associated devices (other Dimmers for example).

Value.label: Assign toggle switch status to the device status

Item.label: Device changes status on switch status change
Device status is synchronized with switch status

Value.label: Double click option
Value.Help: Double-click set lighting at 100%. Default 1.

Value.label: Command frames sent in 2-nd and 3-rd association groups (S1 associations)
Value.Help: Parameter determines, which actions will not result in sending frames to association groups. Parameter values may be combined, e.g. 1+2=3 means that associations on switching on or off the Dimmer (single click) will not be sent. Available settings: 0-31 0 - all actions send to association groups; 1 - do not send when switching on the Dimmer (single click); 2 - do not send when switching off the Dimmer (single click); 4 - do not send when changing dimming level (holding and releasing); 8 - do not send on double click; 16 - send 0xFF value on double click; Default setting: 0

Value.label: Command frames sent in 4-th and 5-th association groups (S2 associations)
Value.Help: Parameter determines, which actions will not result in sending frames to association groups. Parameter values may be combined, e.g. 1+2=3 means that associations on switching on or off the Dimmer (single click) will not be sent. Available settings: 0-31 0 - all actions send to association groups; 1 - do not send when switching on the Dimmer (single click); 2 - do not send when switching off the Dimmer (single click); 4 - do not send when changing dimming level (holding and releasing); 8 - do not send on double click; 16 - send 0xFF value on double click; Default setting: 0

Value.label: The function of 3-way switch
Value.Help: Switch no. 2 controls the Dimmer additionally (in 3-way switch mode). Function disabled for parameter 20 set to 2 (roller blind switch)

Value.label: Associations in Z-Wave network security mode
Value.Help: This parameter defines how commands are sent in specified association groups: as secure or non-secure. Parameter is active only in Z-Wave network security mode. It does not apply to 1st Lifeline group. Parameter values may be combined, e.g. 1+2=3 means that 2nd & 3rd group are sent as secure. Available settings: 0-15 0 - all groups (II-V) sent as non-secure; 1 - 2nd group sent as secure; 2 - 3rd group sent as secure; 4 - 4th group sent as secure; 8 - 5th group sent as secure; 15 - all groups (II-V) sent as secure; Default setting: 15

Value.label: Scene activation functionality
Value.Help: SCENE ID depends on the switch type configurations. Default 0.

Value.label: Switch functionality of S1 and S2

Item.label: S1 operates as S2, S2 operates as S1

Value.label: Load control mode
Value.Help: Forced auto-calibration will set this parameter value to 2.

Item.label: control mode selected automatically (based on auto-calibration)

Value.label: Load control mode
Value.Help: Load control mode recognized during auto-calibration.

Value.label: On/Off mode
Value.Help: Load control mode recognized during auto-calibration.

Item.label: on/off mode enabled (dimming is not possible)

Value.label: Dimmability of the load
Value.Help: Dimmability of the load

Value.label: Soft-Start functionality
Value.Help: Time required to warm up the filament of halogen bulb.

Value.label: Auto-calibration after power on
Value.Help: This parameter determines the trigger of auto-calibration procedure, e.g. power on, load error, etc.

Item.label: No auto-calibration of the load after power on
Auto-calibration performed after first power on
Auto-calibration performed after each power on
Auto-calibration performed after first power on or after each LOAD ERROR alarm (no load, load failure, burnt out bulb)
Auto-calibration performed after each power on or after each LOAD ERROR alarm (no load, load failure, burnt out bulb),

Value.label: Behaviour of the Dimmer after OVERCURRENT or SURGE
Value.Help: Behaviour of the Dimmer after OVERCURRENT or SURGE

Item.label: device permanently disabled until re-enabling by comand or external switch
three attempts to turn on the load

Value.label: Brightness level correction for flickering loads
Value.Help: Correction reduces spontaneous flickering of some capacitive load (e.g. dimmable lEDs) at certain brightness levels in 2-wire installation. In countries using ripple-control, correction may cause changes in brightness. In this case it is necessary to disable correction or adjust time of correction for flickering loads. Available settings: 0 - automatic correction disabled 1-254 - duration of correction in seconds 255 - automatic correction always enabled Default setting: 255

Value.label: Power limit - OVERLOAD
Value.Help: Reaching the defined value will result in turning off the load. Additional apparent power limit of 350VA is active by default. Available settings: 0 - functionality disabled; 1-350 - 1W-350W; Default setting: 250

Value.label: Response to General Purpose Alarm
Value.Help: Response to General Purpose Alarm; Default 3.

Item.label: No reaction - no response to alarm frames
ALARM DIMMER ON - device turn ON upon receipt of alarm frame
ALARM DIMMER OFF - device will turn OFF upon receipt of alarm frame
ALARM FLASHING - device will turn ON and OFF periodically

Value.label: Response to Water Flooding Alarm
Value.Help: Response to Water Flooding Alarm; Default 2.

Item.label: No reaction - no response to alarm frames
ALARM DIMMER ON - device turn ON upon receipt of alarm frame
ALARM DIMMER OFF - device will turn OFF upon receipt of alarm frame
ALARM FLASHING - device will turn ON and OFF periodically

Value.label: Response to Smoke, CO or CO2 Alarm
Value.Help: Response to Smoke, CO or CO2 Alarm; Default 3.

Item.label: No reaction - no response to alarm frames
ALARM DIMMER ON - device turn ON upon receipt of alarm frame
ALARM DIMMER OFF - device will turn OFF upon receipt of alarm frame
ALARM FLASHING - device will turn ON and OFF periodically

Value.label: Response to Temperature Alarm
Value.Help: Response to Temperature Alarm; Default 1.

Item.label: No reaction - no response to alarm frames
ALARM DIMMER ON - device turn ON upon receipt of alarm frame
ALARM DIMMER OFF - device will turn OFF upon receipt of alarm frame
ALARM FLASHING - device will turn ON and OFF periodically

Value.label: Time of alarm state
Value.Help: Alarm state may be cancelled earlier, as a result of pressing the switches or sending the Z-Wave command frame.

Value.label: OVERLOAD alarm report
Value.Help: OVERLOAD alarm report (load power consumption too high)

Value.label: LOAD ERROR alarm report
Value.Help: LOAD ERROR alarm report (no load, load failure, burnt out bulb)

Value.label: OVERCURRENT alarm report
Value.Help: OVERCURRENT alarm report (short circuit, burnt out bulb causing overcurrent)

Value.label: SURGE alarm report
Value.Help: SURGE alarm report (Dimmer output overvoltage)

Value.label: OVERHEAT and VOLTAGE DROP alarm report
Value.Help: OVERHEAT (critical temperature) and VOLTAGE DROP (low voltage) alarm report

Value.label: Active power reports
Value.Help: The parameter defines the power level change that will result in a new power report being sent. The value is a percentage of the previous report. Available settings: 0 - power reports disabled; 1-100 (1-100%) - power report threshold; Default setting: 10 (10%)

Value.label: Periodic active power and energy reports
Value.Help: Parameter 52 defines a time period between consecutive reports. Timer is reset and counted from zero after each report. Available settings: 0 - periodic reports disabled; 1-32767 (1-32767 seconds); Default setting: 3600 (3600s)

Value.label: Energy reports
Value.Help: Energy level change which will result in sending a new energy report. Available settings: 0 - energy reports disabled; 1-255 (0,01 - 2,55 kWh) - report triggering threshold; Default setting: 10 (0,1 kWh)

Value.label: Self-measurement
Value.Help: The Dimmer may include active power and energy consumed by itself in reports sent to the main controller.

Value.label: Method of calculating the active power
Value.Help: This parameter defines how to calculate active power. This parameter is useful in a case of 2-wire connection with light sources other than resistive. The parameter is set to 0 after forced auto-calibration.

Item.label: power measurement based on the standard algorithm
approximation based on the calibration data
approximation based on the control angle

Value.label: Approximated power at the maximum brightness level
Value.Help: This parameter determines the approximate value of the power that will be reported by the device at its maximum brightness level. This parameter works only when parameter 58 has a value other than 0. Available settings: 0-500 (0-500W) - power consumed by the load at the maximum brightness level. Default setting: 0

Value.label: Report type
Value.Help: Defines the contents of Multilevel Sensor Report after Multilevel Sensor Get received.

Value.label: Blinking behavior
Value.Help: This is actually a double byte value. The LSB defines the total time the device needs to blink in seconds. The MSB defines the on/off interval of the blink in tenths of seconds.

Value.label: Notification status
Value.Help: Enables automatic notifications to associated devices whenever there is a state change.

Value.label: Enable Wattage Reports
Value.Help: Enable/disable Wattage threshold and percent.

Value.label: Wattage threshold
Value.Help: The minimum change in wattage for a report to be sent.

Value.label: Wattage threshold percent
Value.Help: Minimum change in percent of wattage to send a report.

Value.label: Default Group Reports
Value.Help: Set report types for groups 1, 2 and 3 to default. Reverts to 0 after set.

Value.label: Report type G1
Value.Help: Defines the type of report sent for reporting group 1. 2 is multisensor report. 4 is meter report for watts. 8 is meter report for kilowatts. Value 1 (msb) Reserved Value 2 Reserved Value 3 Reserved Value 4 (lsb) bits 7-4 reserved bit 3 MRC(KWH) bit 2 MRC(watt) bit 1 MSRC bit 0 reserved The MRC(KWH) flag signals that Report Group 1 send(1) or don't send(0) Meter Report Command(KWh) automatically. The MRC(Watt) flag signals that Report Group 1 send(1) or don't send(0) Meter Report Command(wattage) automatically. The MSRC flag signals that Report Group 1 send(1) or don't send(0) Multilevel Sensor Report Command(wattage) automatically.

Value.label: Report type G2
Value.Help: Defines the type of report sent for reporting group 2. 2 is multisensor report. 4 is meter report for watts. 8 is meter report for kilowatts. Value 1 (msb) Reserved Value 2 Reserved Value 3 Reserved Value 4 (lsb) bits 7-4 reserved bit 3 MRC(KWH) bit 2 MRC(watt) bit 1 MSRC bit 0 reserved The MRC(KWH) flag signals that Report Group 1 send(1) or don't send(0) Meter Report Command(KWh) automatically. The MRC(Watt) flag signals that Report Group 1 send(1) or don't send(0) Meter Report Command(wattage) automatically. The MSRC flag signals that Report Group 1 send(1) or don't send(0) Multilevel Sensor Report Command(wattage) automatically.

Value.label: Report type G3
Value.Help: Defines the type of report sent for reporting group 3. 2 is multisensor report. 4 is meter report for watts. 8 is meter report for kilowatts. Value 1 (msb) Reserved Value 2 Reserved Value 3 Reserved Value 4 (lsb) bits 7-4 reserved bit 3 MRC(KWH) bit 2 MRC(watt) bit 1 MSRC bit 0 reserved The MRC(KWH) flag signals that Report Group 1 send(1) or don't send(0) Meter Report Command(KWh) automatically. The MRC(Watt) flag signals that Report Group 1 send(1) or don't send(0) Meter Report Command(wattage) automatically. The MSRC flag signals that Report Group 1 send(1) or don't send(0) Multilevel Sensor Report Command(wattage) automatically.

Value.label: Default Group Time
Value.Help: Set time interval for sending reports for groups 1, 2 and 3 to default.

Value.label: Send interval G1
Value.Help: Defines the time interval when the defined report for group 1 is sent.

Value.label: Send interval G2
Value.Help: Defines the time interval when the defined report for group 2 is sent.

Value.label: Send interval G3
Value.Help: Defines the time interval when the defined report for group 3 is sent.

Value.label: Device tag
Value.Help: Device tag.

Value.label: Reset device
Value.Help: Reset to the default configuration.

Value.label: Enable/Disable ALL ON/OFF
Value.Help: Enable/Disable ALL ON/OFF

Item.label: ALL ON disabled/ ALL OFF disabled
ALL ON disabled/ ALL OFF active
ALL ON active / ALL OFF disabled
ALL ON active / ALL OFF active

Value.label: Auto off relay after specified time
Value.Help: Auto off relay after specified time, with the possibility of manual override - immediate Off after button push. Default value: 0 Manual override disabled

Value.label: Auto off
Value.Help: Time period for auto off, in milliseconds (0,1 s to 6553,5 s) 0 - Auto off disabled Default value: 0

Value.label: Separation of association sending (key 1)
Value.Help: Activate/Deactivate association sending for group 1 - Also see param #15

Item.label: Map status to all devices in group 1 (default)
Map OFF status to all devices in group 1, Double click on key 1 will send ON to all devices in group 1, all dimmers set to prev.value
Map OFF status to all devices in group 1, Double click on key 1 will send ON to all devices in group 1, all dimmers set to 100%

Value.label: Inputs behaviour
Value.Help: In case of bi-stable switches, define their behaviour (toggle or follow)

Value.label: Inputs Button/Switch configuration
Value.Help: Binary inputs type configuration

Value.label: Dimmer/Roller shutter control
Value.Help: Enable/Disable operation of dimmer or roller shutter devices associated to group 1. Available only when using mono-stable inputs (buttons) - Hold button 1 or double-tap for operation

Value.label: Saving state before power failure
Value.Help: Saving state before power failure

Item.label: State NOT saved at power failure, all outputs are set to OFF upon power restore
State saved at power failure, all outputs are set to previous state upon power restore
DEACTIVATION - no response to alarm frames
ALARM RELAY ON - relay will turn ON upon receipt of alarm frame
ALARM RELAY OFF - relay will turn OFF upon receipt of alarm frame
ALARM FLASHING - relay will turn ON and OFF periodically (see param.39)

Value.label: Relay: Response to Water Flood Alarm

Item.label: DEACTIVATION - no response to alarm frames
ALARM RELAY ON - relay will turn ON upon receipt of alarm frame
ALARM RELAY OFF - relay will turn OFF upon receipt of alarm frame
ALARM FLASHING - relay will turn ON and OFF periodically (see param.39)

Value.label: Relay: Response to Smoke, CO, CO2 Alarm

Item.label: DEACTIVATION - no response to alarm frames
ALARM RELAY ON - relay will turn ON upon receipt of alarm frame
ALARM RELAY OFF - relay will turn OFF upon receipt of alarm frame
ALARM FLASHING - relay will turn ON and OFF periodically (see param.39)
DEACTIVATION - no response to alarm frames
ALARM RELAY ON - relay will turn ON upon receipt of alarm frame
ALARM RELAY OFF - relay will turn OFF upon receipt of alarm frame
ALARM FLASHING - relay will turn ON and OFF periodically (see param.39)

Value.label: Active flashing alarm time
Value.Help: Amount of time (ms) the device keeps on flashing after receipt of Alarm Frame

Value.label: Over current protection
Value.Help: Output load will be closed after 30 seconds if the current exceeds (US: 15.5A, AU or EU: 10.5A).

Value.label: Over heat protection
Value.Help: Output load will be closed after 30 seconds if the temperature inside the product exceeds 100C.

Value.label: Status re-power on
Value.Help: Configure the output load status after re-power on

Item.label: The last status before the power outage

Value.label: WallSwipe IR sensor
Value.Help: Enable/disable the IR sensor of WallSwipe. (LSB) Value 1. Value 1 = 0, disable the IR Sensor. Value 1 = 1, enable the IR Sensor. Value 2 = 0, Wave Option 1 is selected. Value 2 = 1, Wave Option 2 is selected. Value 3 = 0, disable the scene control functionality for Left/Right wave. Value 3 = 1, enable the scene control functionality for Left/Right wave Value 4 = 0, disable the scene control functionality for all wave actions. Value 4 = 1, enable the scene control functionality for all wave actions.

Value.label: WallSwipe button color
Value.Help: Set the button color of WallSwipe. Value 1= Level 1. Value 2= the color value of Red. Value 3= the color value of Green. Value 4= the color value of Blue.

Value.label: WallSwipe LED Up gesture action
Value.Help: Set the LED indication color of WallSwipe when the gesture action is UP. Value 1= Reserved. Value 2= the color value of Red. Value 3= the color value of Green. Value 4= the color value of Blue.

Value.label: WallSwipe LED Down gesture action
Value.Help: Set the LED indication color of WallSwipe when the gesture action is Down. Value 1= Reserved. Value 2= the color value of Red. Value 3= the color value of Green. Value 4= the color value of Blue.

Value.label: WallSwipe LED Left gesture action
Value.Help: Set the LED indication color of WallSwipe when the gesture action is Left. Value 1= Reserved. Value 2= the color value of Red. Value 3= the color value of Green. Value 4= the color value of Blue.

Value.label: WallSwipe LED Right gesture action
Value.Help: Set the LED indication color of WallSwipe when the gesture action is Right. Value 1= Reserved. Value 2= the color value of Red. Value 3= the color value of Green. Value 4= the color value of Blue.

Value.label: WallSwipe Night light
Value.Help: Set the LED indication color of WallSwipe when it is in Night light state. Value 1= Reserved. Value 2= the color value of Red. Value 3= the color value of Green. Value 4= the color value of Blue.

Value.label: WallSwipe sensitivity
Value.Help: Set the sensitivity of WallSwipe.

Value.label: Notification report association group 1
Value.Help: To set which notification would be sent to the associated nodes in association group 1 when the state of output load is changed

Item.label: Hail CC when using the external switch to switch the loads

Value.label: Notification report using the external switch 1
Value.Help: To set which notification would be sent to the associated nodes in association group 3 when using the external switch 1 to switch the loads

Value.label: Notification report using the external switch 2
Value.Help: To set which notification would be sent to the associated nodes in association group 4 when using the external switch 2 to switch the loads

Value.label: LED status
Value.Help: Configure the state of LED when it is in 3 modes below

Item.label: The LED will follow the status
LED will follow the status (on/off) of its load but the LED will turn off after 5 seconds

Value.label: Time set for Night light mode
Value.Help: Value1: Hour of ON. Value2: Minute of ON. Value3: Hour of OFF. Value4: Minute of OFF. E.g. The default setting is the Night light mode is enabled at 12: 00 and disabled at 8:00 0x12000800.

Value.label: Set the ON time of output load
Value.Help: Value1: 0= Disable / 1= Enable. Value2: Weekday (Bit 0-6 means Monday to Sunday). Value3: Hour of turning OFF. Value4: Minute of turning OFF E.g. The default setting 0x007F1200

Value.label: Set the OFF time of output load
Value.Help: Value1: 0= Disable / 1= Enable. Value2: Weekday (Bit 0-6 means Monday to Sunday). Value3: Hour of turning OFF. Value4: Minute of turning OFF E.g. The default setting 0x001707F00

Value.label: Enable/disable the parameter 91 and 92
Value.Help: If this parameter is set to 1 (Enable), the parameter 101 - 103 should be set to 0.

Value.label: Minimum Change to send Report (Watt)
Value.Help: The value represents the minimum change in wattage for a Report to be sent (default 25 W)

Value.label: Minimum Change to send Report (%)
Value.Help: The value represents the minimum percentage change in wattage for a Report to be sent (Default 5)

Value.label: Default Group Reports
Value.Help: Set report types for groups 1, 2 and 3 to default.

Value.label: Report type sent in Reporting Group 1
Value.Help: Defines the type of report sent for reporting group 1. 1 is meter report for voltage average. 2 is meter report for current total. 4 is meter report for watts total. 8 is meter report for kilowatts total. Value 1 (msb) Reserved Value 2 bit 4 current S2 bit 3 current S1 bit 2 Reserved bit 1 voltage S2 bit 0 voltage S1 Value 3 bit 4 kWh S2 bit 3 kWh S1 bit 2 Reserved bit 1 watt S2 bit 0 watt S1 Value 4 (lsb) bits 7-4 reserved bit 3 current total bit 2 voltage average bit 1 watt total bit 0 kWh total

Value.label: Report type sent in Reporting Group 2
Value.Help: Defines the type of report sent for reporting group 2. 1 is meter report for voltage average. 2 is meter report for current total. 4 is meter report for watts total. 8 is meter report for kilowatts total. Value 1 (msb) Reserved Value 2 bit 4 current S2 bit 3 current S1 bit 2 Reserved bit 1 voltage S2 bit 0 voltage S1 Value 3 bit 4 kWh S2 bit 3 kWh S1 bit 2 Reserved bit 1 watt S2 bit 0 watt S1 Value 4 (lsb) bits 7-4 reserved bit 3 current total bit 2 voltage average bit 1 watt total bit 0 kWh total

Value.label: Report type sent in Reporting Group 3
Value.Help: Defines the type of report sent for reporting group 3. 1 is meter report for voltage average. 2 is meter report for current total. 4 is meter report for watts total. 8 is meter report for kilowatts total. Value 1 (msb) Reserved Value 2 bit 4 current S2 bit 3 current S1 bit 2 Reserved bit 1 voltage S2 bit 0 voltage S1 Value 3 bit 4 kWh S2 bit 3 kWh S1 bit 2 Reserved bit 1 watt S2 bit 0 watt S1 Value 4 (lsb) bits 7-4 reserved bit 3 current total bit 2 voltage average bit 1 watt total bit 0 kWh total

Value.label: Set 111-113 to default
Value.Help: Set time interval for sending reports for groups 1, 2 and 3 to default.

Value.label: Send Interval for Reporting Group 1
Value.Help: Defines the time interval when the defined report for group 1 is sent.

Value.label: Send Interval for Reporting Group 2
Value.Help: Defines the time interval when the defined report for group 2 is sent.

Value.label: Send Interval for Reporting Group 3
Value.Help: Defines the time interval when the defined report for group 3 is sent.

Value.label: Set the external S1 mode
Value.Help: When the switch mode of S1 is determined or identified or configured, this mode value will not be reset after exclusion

Value.label: Set the external S2 mode
Value.Help: When the switch mode of S2 is determined or identified or configured, this mode value will not be reset after exclusion

Value.label: Control destination for S1
Value.Help: Set the control destination for external switch S1

Item.label: Control the output load itself and other nodes

Value.label: Control destination for S2
Value.Help: Set the control destination for external switch S2

Item.label: Control the output load itself and other nodes

Value.label: WallSwipe
Value.Help: Get the state of touch panel port

Value.label: S1/S2 when the Switch mode is 3-way switch
Value.Help: Set the working way for S1/S2 when the Switch mode is 3-way switch mode.

Item.label: S1 and S2 is a toggle switch
S1 and S2 are an On/Off switch

Value.label: Function of S1/S2
Value.Help: Set the function of S1/S2. Bit 0 = 0, the function of sending NIF is disabled. Bit 0 = 1, the function of sending NIF is enabled. Bit 1 = 0, the function of entering RF power level test mode is disabled. Bit 1 = 1, the function of entering RF power level test mode is enabled. Bit 2 = 0, the function of factory reset is disabled. Bit 2 = 1, the function of factory reset is enabled. Bit 3 to Bit 6 are reserved. Bit 7 = 0, the setting for Bit 0 to Bit 2 are ineffective. Bit 7 = 1, the setting for Bit 0 to Bit 2 are effective.

Value.label: Configuration factory reset function
Value.Help: Enable/disable the factory reset function of Action Button, external switches or WallSwipe.

Value.label: Configuration Locked
Value.Help: Enable/disable Configuration Locked

Value.label: Reset To Factory Defaults
Value.Help: Reset to factory defaults

Item.label: Reset to factory default setting and removed from the z-wave network

Value.label: Switch type
Value.Help: This parameter defines as what type the device should treat the switch connected to the S1 and S2 terminals. This parameter is not relevant in gate operating modes (parameter 151 set to 3 or 4). In this case switch always works as a momentary and has to be connected to S1 terminal.

Value.label: Inputs orientation
Value.Help: This parameter allows reversing the operation of switches connected to S1 and S2 without changing the wiring. Default: S1 -> 1st channel, S2 -> 2nd channel. Reversed: S1 -> 2nd channel, S2 -> 1st channel.

Value.label: Outputs orientation
Value.Help: This parameter allows reversing the operation of Q1 and Q2 without changing the wiring (in case of invalid motor connection) to ensure proper operation. - Default: Q1 -> 1st channel, Q2 -> 2nd channel. - Reversed: Q1 -> 2nd channel, Q2 -> 1st channel.

Value.label: Alarm configuration - 1st slot
Value.Help: This parameter determines to which alarm frames and how the device should react. The parameters consist of 4 bytes, three most significant bytes are set according to the official Z-Wave protocol specification. 1B [MSB] - notification Type. 2B - notification Status. 3B - Event/State Parameters. 4B [lSB] action: 0 - no action, 1 - open blinds, 2 - close blinds. Default setting: [0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00]

Value.label: Alarm configuration - 2st slot (water)
Value.Help: This parameter determines to which alarm frames and how the device should react. The parameters consist of 4 bytes, three most significant bytes are set according to the official Z-Wave protocol specification. 1B [MSB] - notification Type. 2B - notification Status. 3B - Event/State Parameters. 4B [lSB] action: 0 - no action, 1 - open blinds, 2 - close blinds. Default setting: [0x05, 0xFF, 0x00, 0x00] (Water Alarm, any notification, no action)

Value.label: Alarm configuration - 3st slot (smoke)
Value.Help: This parameter determines to which alarm frames and how the device should react. The parameters consist of 4 bytes, three most significant bytes are set according to the official Z-Wave protocol specification. 1B [MSB] - notification Type. 2B - notification Status. 3B - Event/State Parameters. 4B [lSB] action: 0 - no action, 1 - open blinds, 2 - close blinds. Default setting: [0x01, 0xFF, 0x00, 0x00] (Smoke Alarm, any notification, no action)

Value.label: Alarm configuration - 4st slot (CO)
Value.Help: This parameter determines to which alarm frames and how the device should react. The parameters consist of 4 bytes, three most significant bytes are set according to the official Z-Wave protocol specification. 1B [MSB] - notification Type. 2B - notification Status. 3B - Event/State Parameters. 4B [lSB] action: 0 - no action, 1 - open blinds, 2 - close blinds. Default setting: [0x02, 0xFF, 0x00, 0x00] (CO Alarm, any notification, no action)

Value.label: Alarm configuration - 5st slot (heat)
Value.Help: This parameter determines to which alarm frames and how the device should react. The parameters consist of 4 bytes, three most significant bytes are set according to the official Z-Wave protocol specification. 1B [MSB] - notification Type. 2B - notification Status. 3B - Event/State Parameters. 4B [lSB] action: 0 - no action, 1 - open blinds, 2 - close blinds. Default setting: [0x04, 0xFF, 0x00, 0x00] (Heat Alarm, any notification, no action)

Value.label: S1 switch - scenes sent
Value.Help: This parameter determines which actions result in sending scene IDs assigned to them. Sum of: 1 - Key pressed 1 time. 2 - Key pressed 2 times. 4 - Key pressed 3 times. 8 - Key hold down and key released. Default setting: 0.

Value.label: S2 switch - scenes sent
Value.Help: This parameter determines which actions result in sending scene IDs assigned to them. Sum of: 1 - Key pressed 1 time. 2 - Key pressed 2 times. 4 - Key pressed 3 times. 8 - Key hold down and key released. Default setting: 0.

Value.label: Measuring power consumed by the device itself
Value.Help: This parameter determines whether the power metering should include the amount of active power consumed by the device itself.

Value.label: Power reports - on change
Value.Help: This parameter determines the minimum change in consumed power that will result in sending new power report to the main controller. For loads under 50W, the parameter is not relevant and reports are sent every 5W change. Power reports are sent no often than every 30 seconds. 0: reports are disabled. 1-500 (1-500%): change in power. Default setting: 15.

Value.label: Power reports - periodic
Value.Help: This parameter determines in what time intervals the periodic power reports are sent to the main controller. Periodic reports do not depend on power change (parameter 61). 0: periodic reports are disabled 30-32400 (30-32400s): report interval. Default setting: 3600 (1h).

Value.label: Energy reports - on change
Value.Help: This parameter determines the minimum change in consumed energy that will result in sending new energy report to the main controller. 0: reports are disabled. 1-500 (0.01 - 5 kWh): change in energy. Default setting: 10 (0.1 kWh).

Value.label: Energy reports - periodic
Value.Help: This parameter determines in what time intervals the periodic energy reports are sent to the main controller. Periodic reports do not depend on energy change (parameter 65). 0: periodic reports are disabled. 30-32400 (30-32400s): report interval. Default setting: 3600 (1h)

Value.label: Force calibration
Value.Help: By setting this parameter to 2 the device enters the calibration mode. The parameter relevant only if the device is set to work in positioning mode (parameter 151 set to 1, 2 or 4).

Value.label: Operating mode
Value.Help: This parameter allows adjusting operation according to the connected device.

Value.label: Venetian blind - time of full turn of the slats
Value.Help: For Venetian blinds (parameter 151 set to 2) the parameter determines time of full turn cycle of the slats. For gates (parameter 151 set to 3 or 4) the parameter determines time after which open gate will start closing automatically (if set to 0, gate will not close). The parameter is irrelevant for other modes. 0-90000 (0 - 900s, every 0.01s) time of turn. Default setting: 150 (1.5s).

Value.label: Set slats back to previous position
Value.Help: For Venetian blinds (parameter 151 set to 2) the parameter determines slats positioning in various situations. The parameter is irrelevant for other modes.

Item.label: Only in case of the main controller operation
In case of the main controller operation, momentary switch operation, or when the limit switch is reached.
In case of the main controller operation, momentary switch operation, when the limit switch is reached or after receiving the Switch Multilevel Stop control frame

Value.label: Delay motor stop after reaching end switch
Value.Help: For blinds (parameter 151 set to 1, 2, 5 or 6) the parameter determines the time after which the motor will be stopped after end switch contacts are closed. For gates (parameter 151 set to 3 or 4) the parameter determines the time after which the gate will start closing automatically if S2 contacts are opened (if set to 0, gate will not close). 0-600 (0 - 60s). Default setting: 10 (1s).

Value.label: Motor operation detection
Value.Help: Power threshold to be interpreted as reaching a limit switch. 0: reaching a limit switch will not be detected 1-255 (1-255W): report interval. Default setting: 10.

Value.label: Time of up movement
Value.Help: This parameter determines the time needed for roller blinds to reach the top. For modes with positioning value is set automatically during calibration, otherwise, it must be set manually. 1-90000 (0.01 - 900.00s). Default setting: 6000 (60s).

Value.label: Time of down movement
Value.Help: This parameter determines time needed for roller blinds to reach the bottom. For modes with positioning value is set automatically during calibration, otherwise, it must be set manually. 1-90000 (0.01 - 900.00s). Default setting: 6000 (60s).

Value.label: Saving state before power failure
Value.Help: Saving state before power failure

Item.label: State NOT saved at power failure, all outputs are set to OFF upon power restore
State saved at power failure, all outputs are set to previous state upon power restore

Value.label: First channel - operating mode
Value.Help: This parameter allows to choose operating for the 1st channel controlled by the S1 switch.

Value.label: First channel - reaction to switch for delay/auto ON/OFF modes
Value.Help: This parameter determines how the device in timed mode reacts to pushing the switch connected to the S1 terminal.

Item.label: Cancel mode and set target state
No reaction to switch - mode runs until it ends
Reset timer - start counting from the beginning

Value.label: First channel - time parameter for delay/auto ON/OFF modes
Value.Help: This parameter allows to set time parameter used in timed modes. (s)

Value.label: First channel - pulse time for flashing mode
Value.Help: This parameter allows to set time of switching to opposite state in flashing mode. 1-32000 (0.1-3200.0s, 0.1s step)

Value.label: Second channel - operating mode
Value.Help: This parameter allows to choose operating for the 2st channel controlled by the S2 switch.

Value.label: Second channel - reaction to switch for delay/auto ON/OFF modes
Value.Help: This parameter determines how the device in timed mode reacts to pushing the switch connected to the S2 terminal.

Item.label: Cancel mode and set target state
No reaction to switch - mode runs until it ends
Reset timer - start counting from the beginning

Value.label: Second channel - time parameter for delay/auto ON/OFF modes
Value.Help: This parameter allows to set time parameter used in timed modes. (s)

Value.label: Second channel - pulse time for flashing mode
Value.Help: This parameter allows to set time of switching to opposite state in flashing mode. 1-32000 (0.1-3200.0s, 0.1s step)

Value.label: Switch type
Value.Help: Inputs type configuration

Item.label: Toggle switch (contact closed - On, contact opened - OFF)
Toggle switch (device changes status when switch changes status)

Value.label: Flashing mode report
Value.Help: Enabled / Disabled reports on Flashing mode

Value.label: Associations in Z-Wave network security mode
Value.Help: This parameter defines how commands are sent to specified association groups: as secure or non-secure. Parameter is active only in Z-Wave network security mode. This parameter does not apply to 1st lifeline group. 1 - 2nd group sent as secure. 2 - 3rd group sent as secure. 4 - 4th group sent as secure. 8 - 5th group sent as secure. Default setting: 15 (all)

Value.label: S1 switch - scenes sent
Value.Help: This parameter determines which actions result in sending scene IDs assigned to them. 1 - Key pressed 1 time. 2 - Key pressed 2 times. 4 - Key pressed 3 times. 8 - Key Hold Down and Key Released. Default setting: 0 (none)

Value.label: S2 switch - scenes sent
Value.Help: This parameter determines which actions result in sending scene IDs assigned to them. 1 - Key pressed 1 time. 2 - Key pressed 2 times. 4 - Key pressed 3 times. 8 - Key Hold Down and Key Released. Default setting: 0 (none)

Value.label: S1 associations sent to 2nd and 3rd association groups
Value.Help: This parameter determines which actions are ignored when sending commands to devices associated in 2nd and 3rd association group. All actions are active by default. 1 - ignore turning On with 1 click of the switch. 2 - ignore turning OFF with 1 click of the switch. 4 - ignore holding and releasing the switch. 8 - ignore double click of the switch. Default setting: 0

Value.label: S1 Switch ON value sent to 2nd and 3rd association groups
Value.Help: This parameter determines value sent with Switch On command to devices associated in 2nd and 3rd association group.

Value.label: S1 Switch OFF value sent to 2nd and 3rd association groups
Value.Help: This parameter determines value sent with Switch Off command to devices associated in 2nd and 3rd association group.

Value.label: S1 Switch Double Click value sent to 2nd and 3rd association groups
Value.Help: This parameter determines value sent with Double Click command to devices associated in 2nd and 3rd association group.

Value.label: S2 associations sent to 4th and 5th association groups
Value.Help: This parameter determines which actions are ignored when sending commands to devices associated in 4th and 5th association group. All actions are active by default. 1 - ignore turning On with 1 click of the switch. 2 - ignore turning OFF with 1 click of the switch. 4 - ignore holding and releasing the switch. 8 - ignore double click of the switch. Default setting: 0

Value.label: S2 Switch ON value sent to 4th and 5th association groups
Value.Help: This parameter determines value sent with Switch On command to devices associated in 4th and 5th association group.

Value.label: S2 Switch OFF value sent to 4th and 5th association groups
Value.Help: This parameter determines value sent with Switch Off command to devices associated in 4th and 5th association group.

Value.label: S2 Switch Double Click value sent to 4th and 5th association groups
Value.Help: This parameter determines value sent with Double Click command to devices associated in 4th and 5th association group.

Value.label: Reaction to General Alarm
Value.Help: This parameter determines how the device will react to General Alarm frame.

Value.label: Reaction to Flood Alarm
Value.Help: This parameter determines how the device will react to Flood Alarm frame.

Value.label: Reaction to CO/CO2/Smoke Alarm
Value.Help: This parameter determines how the device will react to CO/CO2/Smoke Alarm frame.

Value.label: Reaction to Heat Alarm
Value.Help: This parameter determines how the device will react to Heat alarm frame.

Value.label: Flashing alarm duration
Value.Help: This parameter allows to set duration of flashing alarm mode. 1-32000 (0.1-32000s, 1s step) Default: 600 (10min)

Value.label: First channel - power reports
Value.Help: This parameter determines the minimum change in consumed power that will result in sending new power report to the main controller. 1-100 (%) Default 20 (20%)

Value.label: First channel - minimal time between power reports
Value.Help: This parameter determines minimum time that has to elapse before sending new power report to the main controller. 1-120 (s) Default 10 (10s)

Value.label: First channel - energy reports
Value.Help: This parameter determines the minimum change in consumed energy that will result in sending new energy report to the main controller. 1-32000 (0.01-320 kWh) Default 100 (1kWh)

Value.label: Periodic power reports
Value.Help: This parameter determines in what time interval the periodic power reports are sent to the main controller. 1-32000 (s) Default 3600 (1h)

Value.label: Periodic energy reports
Value.Help: This parameter determines in what time interval the periodic power reports are sent to the main controller. 1-32000 (s) Default 3600 (1h)

Value.label: Measuring energy consumed by the device itself
Value.Help: This parameter determines whether energy metering should include the amount of energy consumed by the device itself. Results are being added to energy reports for first endpoint.

Value.label: Saving state before power failure
Value.Help: Saving state before power failure

Item.label: State NOT saved at power failure, all outputs are set to OFF upon power restore
State saved at power failure, all outputs are set to previous state upon power restore

Value.label: Flashing alarm duration
Value.Help: This parameter allows to set duration of flashing alarm mode. 1-32000 (0.1-32000s, 1s step) Default: 600 (10min)

Value.label: First channel - power reports
Value.Help: This parameter determines the minimum change in consumed power that will result in sending new power report to the main controller. 1-100 (%) Default 20 (20%)

Value.label: First channel - minimal time between power reports
Value.Help: This parameter determines minimum time that has to elapse before sending new power report to the main controller. 1-120 (s) Default 10 (10s)

Value.label: First channel - energy reports
Value.Help: This parameter determines the minimum change in consumed energy that will result in sending new energy report to the main controller. 1-32000 (0.01-320 kWh) Default 100 (1kWh)

Value.label: Periodic power reports
Value.Help: This parameter determines in what time interval the periodic power reports are sent to the main controller. 1-32000 (s) Default 3600 (1h)

Value.label: Periodic energy reports
Value.Help: This parameter determines in what time interval the periodic power reports are sent to the main controller. 1-32000 (s) Default 3600 (1h)

Value.label: Measuring energy consumed by the device itself
Value.Help: This parameter determines whether energy metering should include the amount of energy consumed by the device itself. Results are being added to energy reports for first endpoint.

Value.label: Over current protection
Value.Help: Output load will be closed after 30 seconds if the current exceeds (US: 15.5A, AU or EU: 10.5A).

Value.label: Over heat protection
Value.Help: Output load will be closed after 30 seconds if the temperature inside the product exceeds 100C.

Value.label: Status re-power on
Value.Help: Configure the output load status after re-power on

Item.label: The last status before the power outage

Value.label: WallSwipe IR sensor
Value.Help: Enable/disable the IR sensor of WallSwipe. (LSB) Value 1. Value 1 = 0, disable the IR Sensor. Value 1 = 1, enable the IR Sensor. Value 2 = 0, Wave Option 1 is selected. Value 2 = 1, Wave Option 2 is selected. Value 3 = 0, disable the scene control functionality for Left/Right wave. Value 3 = 1, enable the scene control functionality for Left/Right wave Value 4 = 0, disable the scene control functionality for all wave actions. Value 4 = 1, enable the scene control functionality for all wave actions.

Value.label: WallSwipe button color
Value.Help: Set the button color of WallSwipe. Value 1= Level 1. Value 2= the color value of Red. Value 3= the color value of Green. Value 4= the color value of Blue.

Value.label: WallSwipe LED Up gesture action
Value.Help: Set the LED indication color of WallSwipe when the gesture action is UP. Value 1= Reserved. Value 2= the color value of Red. Value 3= the color value of Green. Value 4= the color value of Blue.

Value.label: WallSwipe LED Down gesture action
Value.Help: Set the LED indication color of WallSwipe when the gesture action is Down. Value 1= Reserved. Value 2= the color value of Red. Value 3= the color value of Green. Value 4= the color value of Blue.

Value.label: WallSwipe LED Left gesture action
Value.Help: Set the LED indication color of WallSwipe when the gesture action is Left. Value 1= Reserved. Value 2= the color value of Red. Value 3= the color value of Green. Value 4= the color value of Blue.

Value.label: WallSwipe LED Right gesture action
Value.Help: Set the LED indication color of WallSwipe when the gesture action is Right. Value 1= Reserved. Value 2= the color value of Red. Value 3= the color value of Green. Value 4= the color value of Blue.

Value.label: WallSwipe Night light
Value.Help: Set the LED indication color of WallSwipe when it is in Night light state. Value 1= Reserved. Value 2= the color value of Red. Value 3= the color value of Green. Value 4= the color value of Blue.

Value.label: WallSwipe sensitivity
Value.Help: Set the sensitivity of WallSwipe.

Value.label: Notification report association group 1
Value.Help: To set which notification would be sent to the associated nodes in association group 1 when the state of output load is changed

Item.label: Hail CC when using the external switch to switch the loads

Value.label: Notification report using the external switch 1
Value.Help: To set which notification would be sent to the associated nodes in association group 3 when using the external switch 1 to switch the loads

Value.label: Notification report using the external switch 2
Value.Help: To set which notification would be sent to the associated nodes in association group 4 when using the external switch 2 to switch the loads

Value.label: LED status
Value.Help: Configure the state of LED when it is in 3 modes below

Item.label: The LED will follow the status
LED will follow the status (on/off) of its load but the LED will turn off after 5 seconds

Value.label: Time set for Night light mode
Value.Help: Value1: Hour of ON. Value2: Minute of ON. Value3: Hour of OFF. Value4: Minute of OFF. E.g. The default setting is the Night light mode is enabled at 12: 00 and disabled at 8:00 0x12000800.

Value.label: Set the ON time of output load
Value.Help: Value1: 0= Disable / 1= Enable. Value2: Weekday (Bit 0-6 means Monday to Sunday). Value3: Hour of turning OFF. Value4: Minute of turning OFF E.g. The default setting 0x007F1200

Value.label: Set the OFF time of output load
Value.Help: Value1: 0= Disable / 1= Enable. Value2: Weekday (Bit 0-6 means Monday to Sunday). Value3: Hour of turning OFF. Value4: Minute of turning OFF E.g. The default setting 0x001707F00

Value.label: Enable/disable the parameter 91 and 92
Value.Help: If this parameter is set to 1 (Enable), the parameter 101 - 103 should be set to 0.

Value.label: Minimum Change to send Report (Watt)
Value.Help: The value represents the minimum change in wattage for a Report to be sent (default 25 W)

Value.label: Minimum Change to send Report (%)
Value.Help: The value represents the minimum percentage change in wattage for a Report to be sent (Default 5)

Value.label: Default Group Reports
Value.Help: Set report types for groups 1, 2 and 3 to default.

Value.label: Report type sent in Reporting Group 1
Value.Help: Defines the type of report sent for reporting group 1. 1 is meter report for voltage average. 2 is meter report for current total. 4 is meter report for watts total. 8 is meter report for kilowatts total. Value 1 (msb) Reserved Value 2 bit 4 current S2 bit 3 current S1 bit 2 Reserved bit 1 voltage S2 bit 0 voltage S1 Value 3 bit 4 kWh S2 bit 3 kWh S1 bit 2 Reserved bit 1 watt S2 bit 0 watt S1 Value 4 (lsb) bits 7-4 reserved bit 3 current total bit 2 voltage average bit 1 watt total bit 0 kWh total

Value.label: Report type sent in Reporting Group 2
Value.Help: Defines the type of report sent for reporting group 2. 1 is meter report for voltage average. 2 is meter report for current total. 4 is meter report for watts total. 8 is meter report for kilowatts total. Value 1 (msb) Reserved Value 2 bit 4 current S2 bit 3 current S1 bit 2 Reserved bit 1 voltage S2 bit 0 voltage S1 Value 3 bit 4 kWh S2 bit 3 kWh S1 bit 2 Reserved bit 1 watt S2 bit 0 watt S1 Value 4 (lsb) bits 7-4 reserved bit 3 current total bit 2 voltage average bit 1 watt total bit 0 kWh total

Value.label: Report type sent in Reporting Group 3
Value.Help: Defines the type of report sent for reporting group 3. 1 is meter report for voltage average. 2 is meter report for current total. 4 is meter report for watts total. 8 is meter report for kilowatts total. Value 1 (msb) Reserved Value 2 bit 4 current S2 bit 3 current S1 bit 2 Reserved bit 1 voltage S2 bit 0 voltage S1 Value 3 bit 4 kWh S2 bit 3 kWh S1 bit 2 Reserved bit 1 watt S2 bit 0 watt S1 Value 4 (lsb) bits 7-4 reserved bit 3 current total bit 2 voltage average bit 1 watt total bit 0 kWh total

Value.label: Set 111-113 to default
Value.Help: Set time interval for sending reports for groups 1, 2 and 3 to default.

Value.label: Send Interval for Reporting Group 1
Value.Help: Defines the time interval when the defined report for group 1 is sent.

Value.label: Send Interval for Reporting Group 2
Value.Help: Defines the time interval when the defined report for group 2 is sent.

Value.label: Send Interval for Reporting Group 3
Value.Help: Defines the time interval when the defined report for group 3 is sent.

Value.label: Set the external S1 mode
Value.Help: When the switch mode of S1 is determined or identified or configured, this mode value will not be reset after exclusion

Value.label: Set the external S2 mode
Value.Help: When the switch mode of S2 is determined or identified or configured, this mode value will not be reset after exclusion

Value.label: Control destination for S1
Value.Help: Set the control destination for external switch S1

Item.label: Control the output load itself and other nodes

Value.label: Control destination for S2
Value.Help: Set the control destination for external switch S2

Item.label: Control the output load itself and other nodes

Value.label: WallSwipe
Value.Help: Get the state of touch panel port

Value.label: S1/S2 when the Switch mode is 3-way switch
Value.Help: Set the working way for S1/S2 when the Switch mode is 3-way switch mode.

Item.label: S1 and S2 is a toggle switch
S1 and S2 are an On/Off switch

Value.label: Function of S1/S2
Value.Help: Set the function of S1/S2. Bit 0 = 0, the function of sending NIF is disabled. Bit 0 = 1, the function of sending NIF is enabled. Bit 1 = 0, the function of entering RF power level test mode is disabled. Bit 1 = 1, the function of entering RF power level test mode is enabled. Bit 2 = 0, the function of factory reset is disabled. Bit 2 = 1, the function of factory reset is enabled. Bit 3 to Bit 6 are reserved. Bit 7 = 0, the setting for Bit 0 to Bit 2 are ineffective. Bit 7 = 1, the setting for Bit 0 to Bit 2 are effective.

Value.label: Configuration factory reset function
Value.Help: Enable/disable the factory reset function of Action Button, external switches or WallSwipe.

Value.label: Configuration Locked
Value.Help: Enable/disable Configuration Locked

Value.label: Reset To Factory Defaults
Value.Help: Reset to factory defaults

Item.label: Reset to factory default setting and removed from the z-wave network

Value.Help: Output load will be closed after 30 seconds if the current exceeds (US: 15.5A, AU or EU: 10.5A).

Value.label: Over heat protection
Value.Help: Output load will be closed after 30 seconds if the temperature inside the product exceeds 100C.

Value.label: Status re-power on
Value.Help: Configure the output load status after re-power on

Value 2 = 1, Wave Option 2 is selected. Value 3 = 0, disable the scene control functionality for Left/Right wave. Value 3 = 1, enable the scene control functionality for Left/Right wave Value 4 = 0, disable the scene control functionality for all wave actions. Value 4 = 1, enable the scene control functionality for all wave actions.

Value.Help: Set the LED indication color of WallSwipe when the gesture action is Left. Value 1= Reserved. Value 2= the color value of Red. Value 3= the color value of Green. Value 4= the color value of Blue.

Value.Help: Set the LED indication color of WallSwipe when the gesture action is Right. Value 1= Reserved. Value 2= the color value of Red. Value 3= the color value of Green. Value 4= the color value of Blue.

Value.Help: Set the LED indication color of WallSwipe when it is in Night light state. Value 1= Reserved. Value 2= the color value of Red. Value 3= the color value of Green. Value 4= the color value of Blue.

Value.label: WallSwipe sensitivity
Value.Help: Set the sensitivity of WallSwipe.

Value.label: Notification report association group 1
Value.Help: To set which notification would be sent to the associated nodes in association group 1 when the state of output load is changed

Item.label: Hail CC when using the external switch to switch the loads

Value.label: Notification report using the external switch 1
Value.Help: To set which notification would be sent to the associated nodes in association group 3 when using the external switch 1 to switch the loads

Value.label: Notification report using the external switch 2
Value.Help: To set which notification would be sent to the associated nodes in association group 4 when using the external switch 2 to switch the loads

Value.label: LED status
Value.Help: Configure the state of LED when it is in 3 modes below

Item.label: The LED will follow the status
LED will follow the status (on/off) of its load but the LED will turn off after 5 seconds

Value.label: Time set for Night light mode
Value.Help: Value1: Hour of ON. Value2: Minute of ON. Value3: Hour of OFF. Value4: Minute of OFF. E.g. The default setting is the Night light mode is enabled at 12: 00 and disabled at 8:00 0x12000800.

Value.label: Set the ON time of output load
Value.Help: Value1: 0= Disable / 1= Enable. Value2: Weekday (Bit 0-6 means Monday to Sunday). Value3: Hour of turning OFF. Value4: Minute of turning OFF E.g. The default setting 0x007F1200

Value.label: Set the OFF time of output load
Value.Help: Value1: 0= Disable / 1= Enable. Value2: Weekday (Bit 0-6 means Monday to Sunday). Value3: Hour of turning OFF. Value4: Minute of turning OFF E.g. The default setting 0x001707F00

Value.label: Enable/disable the parameter 91 and 92
Value.Help: If this parameter is set to 1 (Enable), the parameter 101 - 103 should be set to 0.

Value.label: Minimum Change to send Report (Watt)
Value.Help: The value represents the minimum change in wattage for a Report to be sent (default 25 W)

Value.label: Minimum Change to send Report (%)
Value.Help: The value represents the minimum percentage change in wattage for a Report to be sent (Default 5)

Value.label: Default Group Reports
Value.Help: Set report types for groups 1, 2 and 3 to default.

Value.label: Report type sent in Reporting Group 1
Value.Help: Defines the type of report sent for reporting group 1. 1 is meter report for voltage average. 2 is meter report for current total. 4 is meter report for watts total. 8 is meter report for kilowatts total. Value 1 (msb) Reserved Value 2 bit 4 current S2 bit 3 current S1 bit 2 Reserved bit 1 voltage S2 bit 0 voltage S1 Value 3 bit 4 kWh S2 bit 3 kWh S1 bit 2 Reserved bit 1 watt S2 bit 0 watt S1 Value 4 (lsb) bits 7-4 reserved bit 3 current total bit 2 voltage average bit 1 watt total bit 0 kWh total

Value.label: Report type sent in Reporting Group 2
Value.Help: Defines the type of report sent for reporting group 2. 1 is meter report for voltage average. 2 is meter report for current total. 4 is meter report for watts total. 8 is meter report for kilowatts total. Value 1 (msb) Reserved Value 2 bit 4 current S2 bit 3 current S1 bit 2 Reserved bit 1 voltage S2 bit 0 voltage S1 Value 3 bit 4 kWh S2 bit 3 kWh S1 bit 2 Reserved bit 1 watt S2 bit 0 watt S1 Value 4 (lsb) bits 7-4 reserved bit 3 current total bit 2 voltage average bit 1 watt total bit 0 kWh total

Value.label: Report type sent in Reporting Group 3
Value.Help: Defines the type of report sent for reporting group 3. 1 is meter report for voltage average. 2 is meter report for current total. 4 is meter report for watts total. 8 is meter report for kilowatts total. Value 1 (msb) Reserved Value 2 bit 4 current S2 bit 3 current S1 bit 2 Reserved bit 1 voltage S2 bit 0 voltage S1 Value 3 bit 4 kWh S2 bit 3 kWh S1 bit 2 Reserved bit 1 watt S2 bit 0 watt S1 Value 4 (lsb) bits 7-4 reserved bit 3 current total bit 2 voltage average bit 1 watt total bit 0 kWh total

Value.label: Set 111-113 to default
Value.Help: Set time interval for sending reports for groups 1, 2 and 3 to default.

Value.label: Send Interval for Reporting Group 1
Value.Help: Defines the time interval when the defined report for group 1 is sent.

Value.label: Send Interval for Reporting Group 2
Value.Help: Defines the time interval when the defined report for group 2 is sent.

Value.label: Send Interval for Reporting Group 3
Value.Help: Defines the time interval when the defined report for group 3 is sent.

Value.label: Set the external S1 mode
Value.Help: When the switch mode of S1 is determined or identified or configured, this mode value will not be reset after exclusion

Value.label: Set the external S2 mode
Value.Help: When the switch mode of S2 is determined or identified or configured, this mode value will not be reset after exclusion

Value.label: Control destination for S1
Value.Help: Set the control destination for external switch S1

Item.label: Control the output load itself and other nodes

Value.label: Control destination for S2
Value.Help: Set the control destination for external switch S2

Item.label: Control the output load itself and other nodes

Value.label: WallSwipe
Value.Help: Get the state of touch panel port

Value.label: S1/S2 when the Switch mode is 3-way switch
Value.Help: Set the working way for S1/S2 when the Switch mode is 3-way switch mode.

Item.label: S1 and S2 is a toggle switch
S1 and S2 are an On/Off switch

Value.label: Function of S1/S2
Value.Help: Set the function of S1/S2. Bit 0 = 0, the function of sending NIF is disabled. Bit 0 = 1, the function of sending NIF is enabled. Bit 1 = 0, the function of entering RF power level test mode is disabled. Bit 1 = 1, the function of entering RF power level test mode is enabled. Bit 2 = 0, the function of factory reset is disabled. Bit 2 = 1, the function of factory reset is enabled. Bit 3 to Bit 6 are reserved. Bit 7 = 0, the setting for Bit 0 to Bit 2 are ineffective. Bit 7 = 1, the setting for Bit 0 to Bit 2 are effective.

Value.label: Configuration factory reset function
Value.Help: Enable/disable the factory reset function of Action Button, external switches or WallSwipe.

Value.label: Configuration Locked
Value.Help: Enable/disable Configuration Locked

Value.label: Reset To Factory Defaults
Value.Help: Reset to factory defaults

Item.label: Reset to factory default setting and removed from the z-wave network

Value.label: Over current protection
Value.Help: Output load will be closed after 30 seconds if the current exceeds (US: 15.5A, AU or EU: 10.5A).

Value.label: Over heat protection
Value.Help: Output load will be closed after 30 seconds if the temperature inside the product exceeds 100C.

Value.label: Status re-power on
Value.Help: Configure the output load status after re-power on

Item.label: The last status before the power outage

Value.label: WallSwipe IR sensor
Value.Help: Enable/disable the IR sensor of WallSwipe. (LSB) Value 1. Value 1 = 0, disable the IR Sensor. Value 1 = 1, enable the IR Sensor. Value 2 = 0, Wave Option 1 is selected. Value 2 = 1, Wave Option 2 is selected. Value 3 = 0, disable the scene control functionality for Left/Right wave. Value 3 = 1, enable the scene control functionality for Left/Right wave Value 4 = 0, disable the scene control functionality for all wave actions. Value 4 = 1, enable the scene control functionality for all wave actions.

Value.label: WallSwipe button color
Value.Help: Set the button color of WallSwipe. Value 1= Level 1. Value 2= the color value of Red. Value 3= the color value of Green. Value 4= the color value of Blue.

Value.label: WallSwipe LED Up gesture action
Value.Help: Set the LED indication color of WallSwipe when the gesture action is UP. Value 1= Reserved. Value 2= the color value of Red. Value 3= the color value of Green. Value 4= the color value of Blue.

Value.label: WallSwipe LED Down gesture action
Value.Help: Set the LED indication color of WallSwipe when the gesture action is Down. Value 1= Reserved. Value 2= the color value of Red. Value 3= the color value of Green. Value 4= the color value of Blue.

Value.label: WallSwipe LED Left gesture action
Value.Help: Set the LED indication color of WallSwipe when the gesture action is Left. Value 1= Reserved. Value 2= the color value of Red. Value 3= the color value of Green. Value 4= the color value of Blue.

Value.label: WallSwipe LED Right gesture action
Value.Help: Set the LED indication color of WallSwipe when the gesture action is Right. Value 1= Reserved. Value 2= the color value of Red. Value 3= the color value of Green. Value 4= the color value of Blue.

Value.label: WallSwipe Night light
Value.Help: Set the LED indication color of WallSwipe when it is in Night light state. Value 1= Reserved. Value 2= the color value of Red. Value 3= the color value of Green. Value 4= the color value of Blue.

Value.label: WallSwipe sensitivity
Value.Help: Set the sensitivity of WallSwipe.

Value.label: Notification report association group 1
Value.Help: To set which notification would be sent to the associated nodes in association group 1 when the state of output load is changed

Item.label: Hail CC when using the external switch to switch the loads

Value.label: Notification report using the external switch 1
Value.Help: To set which notification would be sent to the associated nodes in association group 3 when using the external switch 1 to switch the loads

Value.label: Notification report using the external switch 2
Value.Help: To set which notification would be sent to the associated nodes in association group 4 when using the external switch 2 to switch the loads

Value.label: LED status
Value.Help: Configure the state of LED when it is in 3 modes below

Item.label: The LED will follow the status

Value.label: Set the OFF time of output load
Value.Help: Value1: 0= Disable / 1= Enable. Value2: Weekday (Bit 0-6 means Monday to Sunday). Value3: Hour of turning OFF. Value4: Minute of turning OFF E.g. The default setting 0x001707F00

Value.label: Enable/disable the parameter 91 and 92
Value.Help: If this parameter is set to 1 (Enable), the parameter 101 - 103 should be set to 0.

Value.Help: The value represents the minimum change in wattage for a Report to be sent (default 25 W)

Value.label: Minimum Change to send Report (%)
Value.Help: The value represents the minimum percentage change in wattage for a Report to be sent (Default 5)

Value.label: Default Group Reports
Value.Help: Set report types for groups 1, 2 and 3 to default.

Value.label: Report type sent in Reporting Group 1
Value.Help: Defines the type of report sent for reporting group 1. 1 is meter report for voltage average. 2 is meter report for current total. 4 is meter report for watts total. 8 is meter report for kilowatts total. Value 1 (msb) Reserved Value 2 bit 4 current S2 bit 3 current S1 bit 2 Reserved bit 1 voltage S2 bit 0 voltage S1 Value 3 bit 4 kWh S2 bit 3 kWh S1 bit 2 Reserved bit 1 watt S2 bit 0 watt S1 Value 4 (lsb) bits 7-4 reserved bit 3 current total bit 2 voltage average bit 1 watt total bit 0 kWh total

Value.label: Report type sent in Reporting Group 2
Value.Help: Defines the type of report sent for reporting group 2. 1 is meter report for voltage average. 2 is meter report for current total. 4 is meter report for watts total. 8 is meter report for kilowatts total. Value 1 (msb) Reserved Value 2 bit 4 current S2 bit 3 current S1 bit 2 Reserved bit 1 voltage S2 bit 0 voltage S1 Value 3 bit 4 kWh S2 bit 3 kWh S1 bit 2 Reserved bit 1 watt S2 bit 0 watt S1 Value 4 (lsb) bits 7-4 reserved bit 3 current total bit 2 voltage average bit 1 watt total bit 0 kWh total

Value.label: Report type sent in Reporting Group 3
Value.Help: Defines the type of report sent for reporting group 3. 1 is meter report for voltage average. 2 is meter report for current total. 4 is meter report for watts total. 8 is meter report for kilowatts total. Value 1 (msb) Reserved Value 2 bit 4 current S2 bit 3 current S1 bit 2 Reserved bit 1 voltage S2 bit 0 voltage S1 Value 3 bit 4 kWh S2 bit 3 kWh S1 bit 2 Reserved bit 1 watt S2 bit 0 watt S1 Value 4 (lsb) bits 7-4 reserved bit 3 current total bit 2 voltage average bit 1 watt total bit 0 kWh total

Value.label: Set 111-113 to default
Value.Help: Set time interval for sending reports for groups 1, 2 and 3 to default.

Value.label: Send Interval for Reporting Group 1
Value.Help: Defines the time interval when the defined report for group 1 is sent.

Value.label: Send Interval for Reporting Group 2
Value.Help: Defines the time interval when the defined report for group 2 is sent.

Value.label: Send Interval for Reporting Group 3
Value.Help: Defines the time interval when the defined report for group 3 is sent.

Value.label: Set the external S1 mode
Value.Help: When the switch mode of S1 is determined or identified or configured, this mode value will not be reset after exclusion

Value.label: Set the external S2 mode
Value.Help: When the switch mode of S2 is determined or identified or configured, this mode value will not be reset after exclusion

Value.label: Control destination for S1
Value.Help: Set the control destination for external switch S1

Value.Help: Set the control destination for external switch S2

Item.label: Control the output load itself and other nodes

Value.label: WallSwipe
Value.Help: Get the state of touch panel port

Value.label: S1/S2 when the Switch mode is 3-way switch
Value.Help: Set the working way for S1/S2 when the Switch mode is 3-way switch mode.

Item.label: S1 and S2 is a toggle switch
S1 and S2 are an On/Off switch

Value.label: Function of S1/S2
Value.Help: Set the function of S1/S2. Bit 0 = 0, the function of sending NIF is disabled. Bit 0 = 1, the function of sending NIF is enabled. Bit 1 = 0, the function of entering RF power level test mode is disabled. Bit 1 = 1, the function of entering RF power level test mode is enabled. Bit 2 = 0, the function of factory reset is disabled. Bit 2 = 1, the function of factory reset is enabled. Bit 3 to Bit 6 are reserved. Bit 7 = 0, the setting for Bit 0 to Bit 2 are ineffective. Bit 7 = 1, the setting for Bit 0 to Bit 2 are effective.

Value.label: Configuration factory reset function
Value.Help: Enable/disable the factory reset function of Action Button, external switches or WallSwipe.

Value.label: Configuration Locked
Value.Help: Enable/disable Configuration Locked

Value.label: Reset To Factory Defaults
Value.Help: Reset to factory defaults

Item.label: Reset to factory default setting and removed from the z-wave network

Value.label: Switch type
Value.Help: This parameter defines as what type the device should treat the switch connected to the S1 and S2 terminals. This parameter is not relevant in gate operating modes (parameter 151 set to 3 or 4). In this case switch always works as a momentary and has to be connected to S1 terminal.

Value.label: Inputs orientation
Value.Help: This parameter allows reversing the operation of switches connected to S1 and S2 without changing the wiring. Default: S1 -> 1st channel, S2 -> 2nd channel. Reversed: S1 -> 2nd channel, S2 -> 1st channel.

Value.label: Outputs orientation
Value.Help: This parameter allows reversing the operation of Q1 and Q2 without changing the wiring (in case of invalid motor connection) to ensure proper operation. - Default: Q1 -> 1st channel, Q2 -> 2nd channel. - Reversed: Q1 -> 2nd channel, Q2 -> 1st channel.

Value.label: Alarm configuration - 1st slot
Value.Help: This parameter determines to which alarm frames and how the device should react. The parameters consist of 4 bytes, three most significant bytes are set according to the official Z-Wave protocol specification. 1B [MSB] - notification Type. 2B - notification Status. 3B - Event/State Parameters. 4B [lSB] action: 0 - no action, 1 - open blinds, 2 - close blinds. Default setting: [0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00]

Value.label: Alarm configuration - 2st slot (water)
Value.Help: This parameter determines to which alarm frames and how the device should react. The parameters consist of 4 bytes, three most significant bytes are set according to the official Z-Wave protocol specification. 1B [MSB] - notification Type. 2B - notification Status. 3B - Event/State Parameters. 4B [lSB] action: 0 - no action, 1 - open blinds, 2 - close blinds. Default setting: [0x05, 0xFF, 0x00, 0x00] (Water Alarm, any notification, no action)

Value.label: Alarm configuration - 3st slot (smoke)
Value.Help: This parameter determines to which alarm frames and how the device should react. The parameters consist of 4 bytes, three most significant bytes are set according to the official Z-Wave protocol specification. 1B [MSB] - notification Type. 2B - notification Status. 3B - Event/State Parameters. 4B [lSB] action: 0 - no action, 1 - open blinds, 2 - close blinds. Default setting: [0x01, 0xFF, 0x00, 0x00] (Smoke Alarm, any notification, no action)

Value.label: Alarm configuration - 4st slot (CO)
Value.Help: This parameter determines to which alarm frames and how the device should react. The parameters consist of 4 bytes, three most significant bytes are set according to the official Z-Wave protocol specification. 1B [MSB] - notification Type. 2B - notification Status. 3B - Event/State Parameters. 4B [lSB] action: 0 - no action, 1 - open blinds, 2 - close blinds. Default setting: [0x02, 0xFF, 0x00, 0x00] (CO Alarm, any notification, no action)

Value.label: Alarm configuration - 5st slot (heat)
Value.Help: This parameter determines to which alarm frames and how the device should react. The parameters consist of 4 bytes, three most significant bytes are set according to the official Z-Wave protocol specification. 1B [MSB] - notification Type. 2B - notification Status. 3B - Event/State Parameters. 4B [lSB] action: 0 - no action, 1 - open blinds, 2 - close blinds. Default setting: [0x04, 0xFF, 0x00, 0x00] (Heat Alarm, any notification, no action)

Value.label: S1 switch - scenes sent
Value.Help: This parameter determines which actions result in sending scene IDs assigned to them. Sum of: 1 - Key pressed 1 time. 2 - Key pressed 2 times. 4 - Key pressed 3 times. 8 - Key hold down and key released. Default setting: 0.

Value.label: S2 switch - scenes sent
Value.Help: This parameter determines which actions result in sending scene IDs assigned to them. Sum of: 1 - Key pressed 1 time. 2 - Key pressed 2 times. 4 - Key pressed 3 times. 8 - Key hold down and key released. Default setting: 0.

Value.label: Measuring power consumed by the device itself
Value.Help: This parameter determines whether the power metering should include the amount of active power consumed by the device itself.

Value.label: Power reports - on change
Value.Help: This parameter determines the minimum change in consumed power that will result in sending new power report to the main controller. For loads under 50W, the parameter is not relevant and reports are sent every 5W change. Power reports are sent no often than every 30 seconds. 0: reports are disabled. 1-500 (1-500%): change in power. Default setting: 15.

Value.label: Power reports - periodic
Value.Help: This parameter determines in what time intervals the periodic power reports are sent to the main controller. Periodic reports do not depend on power change (parameter 61). 0: periodic reports are disabled 30-32400 (30-32400s): report interval. Default setting: 3600 (1h).

Value.label: Energy reports - on change
Value.Help: This parameter determines the minimum change in consumed energy that will result in sending new energy report to the main controller. 0: reports are disabled. 1-500 (0.01 - 5 kWh): change in energy. Default setting: 10 (0.1 kWh).

Value.label: Energy reports - periodic
Value.Help: This parameter determines in what time intervals the periodic energy reports are sent to the main controller. Periodic reports do not depend on energy change (parameter 65). 0: periodic reports are disabled. 30-32400 (30-32400s): report interval. Default setting: 3600 (1h)

Value.label: Force calibration
Value.Help: By setting this parameter to 2 the device enters the calibration mode. The parameter relevant only if the device is set to work in positioning mode (parameter 151 set to 1, 2 or 4).

Value.label: Operating mode
Value.Help: This parameter allows adjusting operation according to the connected device.

Value.label: Venetian blind - time of full turn of the slats
Value.Help: For Venetian blinds (parameter 151 set to 2) the parameter determines time of full turn cycle of the slats. For gates (parameter 151 set to 3 or 4) the parameter determines time after which open gate will start closing automatically (if set to 0, gate will not close). The parameter is irrelevant for other modes. 0-90000 (0 - 900s, every 0.01s) time of turn. Default setting: 150 (1.5s).

Value.label: Set slats back to previous position
Value.Help: For Venetian blinds (parameter 151 set to 2) the parameter determines slats positioning in various situations. The parameter is irrelevant for other modes.

Item.label: Only in case of the main controller operation
In case of the main controller operation, momentary switch operation, or when the limit switch is reached.
In case of the main controller operation, momentary switch operation, when the limit switch is reached or after receiving the Switch Multilevel Stop control frame

Value.label: Delay motor stop after reaching end switch
Value.Help: For blinds (parameter 151 set to 1, 2, 5 or 6) the parameter determines the time after which the motor will be stopped after end switch contacts are closed. For gates (parameter 151 set to 3 or 4) the parameter determines the time after which the gate will start closing automatically if S2 contacts are opened (if set to 0, gate will not close). 0-600 (0 - 60s). Default setting: 10 (1s).

Value.label: Motor operation detection
Value.Help: Power threshold to be interpreted as reaching a limit switch. 0: reaching a limit switch will not be detected 1-255 (1-255W): report interval. Default setting: 10.

Value.label: Time of up movement
Value.Help: This parameter determines the time needed for roller blinds to reach the top. For modes with positioning value is set automatically during calibration, otherwise, it must be set manually. 1-90000 (0.01 - 900.00s). Default setting: 6000 (60s).

Value.label: Time of down movement
Value.Help: This parameter determines time needed for roller blinds to reach the bottom. For modes with positioning value is set automatically during calibration, otherwise, it must be set manually. 1-90000 (0.01 - 900.00s). Default setting: 6000 (60s).
